# Battleship

> A local Battleship game for Android with no server or network connection required. Built with Kotlin + Jetpack Compose + Material3.

## Overview

Battleship is an Android game implementing the classic naval combat game. One player competes against an AI opponent on a 10×10 grid. Ships are placed manually or auto-deployed. The AI uses a hunt/target algorithm with checkerboard parity for intelligent attack patterns.

## Technology Stack

- Kotlin 2.2.10
- Jetpack Compose (BOM 2026.02.01) + Material3
- Navigation Compose 2.8.4
- ViewModel + StateFlow (MVVM)
- Clean Architecture (domain / presentation)
- No network, no persistence, fully local

## Architecture

- `domain/` — Pure Kotlin models (Ship, Board, ShipType, CellState) and AI (BattleshipAI)
- `presentation/` — Compose screens (Menu, Placement, Game, GameOver), ViewModel, Navigation

## Key Files

- `CLAUDE.md` — Project conventions, architecture, engineering principles
- `app/src/main/java/com/cocode/battleship/domain/ai/BattleshipAI.kt` — Stateless hunt/target AI
- `app/src/main/java/com/cocode/battleship/presentation/game/GameViewModel.kt` — Single shared ViewModel for all screens
- `app/src/main/java/com/cocode/battleship/presentation/navigation/BattleshipNavHost.kt` — Navigation with shared ViewModel
